title: G3 handoff: EXP-01 is positive internal evidence; proceed to independent EXP-03
context: The completed revision-3 EXP-01 receipt was adjudicated against its locked preregistration. The primary result supports the hypothesis in the bound synthetic setting: violations were A 40/96 (41.67%), B 20/99 (20.20%), and C 0/100, with zero safety guardrails. The result survives a worst-case missingness check that counts all five invalid A/B outcomes as compliant. C was clean across both languages and all policy families; the incremental C-over-B effect was concentrated in the exists family. The deterministic report identity and hash were independently rechecked before the private result artifact was committed.
decision: Treat EXP-01 as positive internal G3 evidence and proceed to EXP-03 only through an independent human review path. Do not activate blocking, sign G3, or publish a prevention marketing claim from EXP-01 alone. The private result artifact is committed at 23b3d80; public/external marketing still requires an open-fixture replication and model/version disclosure.
consequences: The EXP-01 assignment loop is closed and must not be rerun or extended after seeing results.
The five invalid outcomes remain visible and unimputed.
EXP-03 independence and seven-day follow-up remain unresolved.
Blocking activation and final G3 sign-off remain separate explicit human decisions.
alternatives_rejected: Call EXP-01 inconclusive because of differential invalid outcomes; the worst-case missingness analysis still leaves A at 40% and B at 20% versus C at 0%.
Generalize the result to every repository and model; the bank is synthetic, isolated, and bound to one model/provider version.
Use the result for public prevention marketing; the Constitution requires an external/open-fixture replication.
Continue sampling after 300 outcomes; the preregistered stopping rule is complete and post-result extension would be invalid.
